title: State Fundamentals

learningObjectives.text: Explain what state is using a new example and justify the result.
Apply how state updates work using a new example and justify the result.
Explain state guidelines using a new example and justify the result.
Compare state versus props using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: Learners frequently use state fundamentals without a clear model of why it behaves as it does; this lesson makes that model explicit. It belongs to the **State and Thinking in React** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **What state is** → **How state updates work** → **State guidelines** → **State versus props**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: What state is
sections.body: State is component memory that influences rendering. Each render receives a snapshot; a setter schedules a future render rather than mutating the current snapshot.

sections.title: How state updates work
sections.body: State setters enqueue transitions. When the next value depends on the previous one, an updater function lets queued changes compose correctly.

sections.title: State guidelines
sections.body: Store the minimum changing information. Avoid redundant or contradictory state and choose the nearest owner shared by every consumer.

sections.title: State versus props
sections.body: Props are parent-owned inputs; state is component or shared-owner memory. Do not copy props into state unless independent change is genuinely required.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: State stores component memory and an updater derives the next value.

commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.
summary: State Fundamentals connects What state is, How state updates work, State guidelines.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

questions.prompt: How much does the count increase after one click?

choices.label: React ignores every second state call.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the State Fundamentals rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Both updates calculate from the same render snapshot, so the button increases by one rather than two.
choices.feedback: Both updates calculate from the same render snapshot, so the button increases by one rather than two.

choices.label: State values are mutated before the handler starts.

questions.explanation: Both updates calculate from the same render snapshot, so the button increases by one rather than two.
questions.hint: Trace the actual values, references, call form, render cycle, request flow, or server/client boundary before choosing.

questions.prompt: Which change is the best fix?

choices.label: Use updater functions twice: `setCount(value => value + 1)` so each update receives the previous queued value.
choices.feedback: Use updater functions twice: `setCount(value => value + 1)` so each update receives the previous queued value.

choices.label: Call `setCount` during render until it reaches two.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the State Fundamentals rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Mutate `count++` directly.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the State Fundamentals rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Store the count in a plain local variable.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the State Fundamentals rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: Use updater functions twice: `setCount(value => value + 1)` so each update receives the previous queued value.
questions.hint: Choose the smallest change that fixes the underlying model, not a workaround that hides the symptom.

questions.prompt: Which guideline shows the best engineering judgment about State Fundamentals?

choices.label: Choose the implementation that makes the State Fundamentals behavior explicit, handles the relevant edge case, and can be verified without hidden mutation or timing assumptions.
choices.feedback: Choose the implementation that makes the State Fundamentals behavior explicit, handles the relevant edge case, and can be verified without hidden mutation or timing assumptions.

choices.label: Use State Fundamentals in every file because more abstraction always improves software.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the State Fundamentals rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Avoid State Fundamentals completely because every additional concept reduces maintainability.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the State Fundamentals rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Choose only by the number of lines and ignore correctness, ownership, and future change.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the State Fundamentals rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: Choose the implementation that makes the State Fundamentals behavior explicit, handles the relevant edge case, and can be verified without hidden mutation or timing assumptions.
questions.hint: Choose the option that balances correctness, clarity, cost, and the actual requirement.

assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
